title: mentha × niliaca subsp. velutina
intro: Mentha × niliaca subsp. velutina, also known as velvet peppermint, is a subspecies of the hybrid mint plant Mentha × niliaca, which is a cross between watermint (Mentha aquatica) and spearmint (Mentha spicata). This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of velvet peppermint include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and even alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Velvet peppermint can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and herbal preparations.
